title: adesmia brachysemeon
intro: Adesmia brachysemeon, also known as the Puna Pea, is a flowering plant native to the Andes mountain range in South America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of respiratory infections, and it has also been used to treat digestive issues and skin conditions. Adesmia brachysemeon is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, with the leaves and flowers being used to create a herbal remedy.
